content: Treatment process: leukapheresis collects T-cells; manufacturing takes 2-4 weeks; bridging therapy may be needed; lymphodepleting chemotherapy (fludarabine + cyclophosphamide) x3 days before infusion; CAR-T infusion (single dose); monitoring in certified center for ≥7 days post-infusion. Efficacy: DLBCL - overall response rate (ORR) 50-80%, complete response (CR) 40-60% (ZUMA-1, JULIET, TRANSCEND trials); ALL - CR 60-90% in children/young adults (ELIANA trial); myeloma - ORR 70-95% with BCMA-targeted CAR-T (CARTITUDE, KarMMa). Toxicities: cytokine release syndrome (CRS) - fever, hypotension, hypoxia, graded 1-4, treated with tocilizumab (IL-6 receptor antagonist) and steroids; immune effector cell-associated neurotoxicity syndrome (ICANS) - confusion, aphasia, seizures, cerebral edema, treated with steroids; prolonged cytopenias; hypogammaglobulinemia requiring IVIG; infection risk. REMS program required for prescribing centers.
